descriptions: Bandidos absorbed Ghostrider's chapters by patching them over.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos expanded into Germany in June 2000 when the club "patched over" seventeen chapters of the Ghostrider's, Road Eagles and Destroyers that had been serving as probationary chapters since the previous year.

descriptions: The Schuele Boys are rivals with the East Ferry Street Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: East Ferry Street Gang

descriptions: Camorra Contini clan cooperated with Ukrainians in cigarette smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years, the Camorra Contini clan is said to have cooperated in the same scheme with the Ukrainians.

descriptions: Pecoraro-Renna clashed with the De Feo clan.
edges.evidence_quote: I Pecoraro-Renna si scontrarono con il clan De Feo di Bellizzi, appartenente alla Nuova Camorra Organizzata.

descriptions: After conflict, Pecoraro-Renna and De Feo formed an alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Dopo i pesanti contrasti, i Pecoraro-Renna ed i De Feo hanno stretto una alleanza

descriptions: Sureños list Pandilla Tiny Rascal as enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Pandilla Tiny Rascal

descriptions: The College Kidnappers targeted members of the Al Capone mob for kidnapping.
edges.evidence_quote: Most of the victims snatched by The College Kidnappers were a part of the Al Capone mob.

Atomwaffen Division.description: International far-right extremist and neo-Nazi network formed in 2015 and based in the Southern United States. It expanded across the US and other countries, described as one of the most violent neo-Nazi movements of the 21st century. Designated as a terrorist group by multiple governments, its members were responsible for murders, bombings, and planned attacks. The group dissolved in November 2024.

descriptions: Atomwaffen members play key roles in organizing the Active Club Network, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen Division members have been active 'and play key roles' in organizing the Active Club Network

descriptions: Infobox lists Active Club Network as an ally of Atomwaffen Division.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Renaissance Society (prison) , Azov Battalion , Active Club Network , Combat 18 , Nordic Resistance Movement , Order of Nine Angles , Russian Imperial Movement , Rusich Group , The Base

descriptions: Atomwaffen Division lists Active Club Network as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Active Club Network

descriptions: Active Club Network is listed as an ally of Atomwaffen Division in the infobox.

descriptions: Aryan Renaissance Society is listed as an ally of Atomwaffen Division in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Renaissance Society (prison)

descriptions: Atomwaffen Division and Black Order cooperated to form a border patrol militia.
edges.evidence_quote: Atomwaffen and Finnish lodge of the Black Order also formed a border patrol militia in co-operation, recruiting former military police.

edges.evidence_quote: the Neo-fascist and Neo-Nazi web forum IronMarch.org, which, prior to its shutdown in 2017, had been linked to several acts of Neo-Nazi terrorism and violent militant groups such as ... CasaPound

descriptions: A Bandidos member murdered three members of the Vanløse Group.
edges.evidence_quote: A Bandidos member holding the rank of krigsminister (“minister for war”) within the club was convicted of the 11 November 2015 triple murder of Philip Rasmussen, Suhaib Jaffar, and Mike Patrick Winther, members of a gang known as the Vanløse Group who were killed with a revolver and a shotgun as they slept in their apartment in Frederiksberg, Copenhagen.

descriptions: The Daniel clan and Lyons Crime Family are involved in a violent vendetta.
edges.evidence_quote: Glasgow's crime families are often involved in violent feuds, as with the vendetta between the Daniel clan and the Lyons Crime Family.

Imperial Klans of America.description: White supremacist, white nationalist, neo-Nazi paramilitary organization based in Dawson Springs, Kentucky, founded in 1996 by Ron Edwards. It was the second largest Klan group in the United States until the late 2000s, and briefly the largest in the early 2000s. Weakened by a 2008 judgment and Edwards' 2011 imprisonment, it remained active as of 2020 but had dwindled to two chapters by 2012.

descriptions: IKA founder Ron Edwards joined the Federation of Klans before founding IKA.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 1994, he joined the short-lived Federation of Klans after it broke away from the Knights of the KKK

edges.target: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan
descriptions: IKA founder Ron Edwards was previously head of a klavern of the Knights of the Ku Klux Klan before breaking away.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 1994, he joined the short-lived Federation of Klans after it broke away from the Knights of the KKK

descriptions: Supreme White Alliance assisted IKA with hate rock gatherings but broke ties after Edwards' arrest.
edges.evidence_quote: the Supreme White Alliance, a white power skinhead group that had been assisting the IKA with annual hate rock gatherings, decided to break ties with the IKA after Edwards' arrest.

Indo-Canadian organized crime.description: Indo-Canadian organized crime consists predominantly of young adults and teenagers of Indian ethnic background, primarily Punjabi-Canadians. These groups are among the top five homegrown organized crime hierarchies in Canada, ranked third after Chinese Triads and Hells Angels. They are involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, money laundering, murder, and extortion. The organization emerged in the 1980s, partly fueled by Sikh separatist movements supporting Khalistan.

descriptions: Indo-Canadian organized crime is ranked below Hells Angels in hierarchy.
edges.evidence_quote: Collectively, these groups are among the top 5 major homegrown organized crime hierarchy across the nation in Canada coming in 3rd place, after the Chinese Triads and Hells Angels.

descriptions: Indo-Canadian organized crime is ranked below Chinese Triads in hierarchy.
edges.evidence_quote: Collectively, these groups are among the top 5 major homegrown organized crime hierarchy across the nation in Canada coming in 3rd place, after the Chinese Triads and Hells Angels.

descriptions: Murder Inc. Records is named after the criminal collective Murder, Inc.
edges.evidence_quote: Murder Inc. Records , founded by Irv Gotti , is named after the criminal collective.

descriptions: The Corbi family functionally headed the Baltimore Crew after 1920.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corbi family acquiesced to the Gambino relationship, but maintained local leaders, simply answering to and accessing support from Morici and his New York Gambino connections.

descriptions: The Corbi family cooperated with the Gambino family, answering to and accessing support from them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corbi family acquiesced to the Gambino relationship, but maintained local leaders, simply answering to and accessing support from Morici and his New York Gambino connections.

Preman.description: Preman is a term for Indonesian organized crime groups, ranging from street-level criminals to crime bosses. The word originates from the Dutch 'vrijman' (free man) and refers to thugs often associated with violence and criminality. Historically, preman have acted as enforcers for political authorities, with roots in the Mataram kingdom. They are distinct from the romanticized 'jago' strongman figure.

descriptions: API had personal links to the underworld (preman) and collaborated with them.
edges.evidence_quote: the Jakarta underworld were also a part of the API (Angkatan Pemuda Indonesia, Youth Generation of Indonesia) constituency.

descriptions: BARA worked with local jagos (preman) during the revolution.
edges.evidence_quote: Through BARA (Barisan Rakyat, People's Brigade), they worked with the local jagos.

descriptions: Gerindo involved Jakarta's underworld (preman) to maintain popular credentials.
edges.evidence_quote: the underworld was once again involved in the activities of the Gerindo

descriptions: HAMOT recruited local bosses (preman) for intelligence and auxiliary roles.
edges.evidence_quote: Local bosses such as Panji, Fakhruddin, Harun Umar and Sujono took up service under the Dutch auxiliary force.

descriptions: PKI allied with Jakarta's underworld (preman) for support and protection.
edges.evidence_quote: The local gangs were assets to the PKI as they provided the organizational base...

descriptions: LRJR collaborated with local jagos (preman) like Haji Darip.
edges.evidence_quote: The formation of the LRJR (Lasykar Rakyat Jakarta Raya, People's Militia of Greater Jakarta) once again saw their collaboration with the local jagos like Haji Darip, Pak Macem and Kyai Haji Nurali

descriptions: PETA challenged the power of local gangs (preman).
edges.evidence_quote: the underworld's position was challenged indirectly by the Japanese's construction of a rival institution in the form of Pembela Tanah Air (PETA)

descriptions: Sarekat Islam recruited local jago (preman) for thuggery.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarekat Islam acquired its own access of thuggery through the recruitment of local jago.

Birmingham Zulu Warriors.description: Football hooligan firm associated with Birmingham City F.C., formed in 1982 from an amalgamation of other firms including Apex. Known for its multi-ethnic membership, unusual among hooligan firms. Engages in violent clashes with rival fans, particularly Aston Villa and Wolverhampton Wanderers. Still active as of the article's last update.

descriptions: The two firms have clashed repeatedly.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hardcore have clashed a number of times with the Zulu Warriors , associated with Aston Villa's fiercest rivals Birmingham City .

descriptions: Zulu Warriors formed from amalgamation of Apex and other firms.
edges.evidence_quote: The Zulu Warriors were formed in 1982 out of an amalgamation of other Birmingham City firms including Apex.

descriptions: Brew Crew is an offshoot gang created from the Zulu Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: The Zulu Warriors have also seen offshoot gangs created such as the Brew Crew and the Junior Business Boys

descriptions: In the film Green Street, the Zulu Warriors fight the Inter City Firm (as Green Street Elite).
edges.evidence_quote: The match shown in the film is supposedly between West Ham United F.C. and Birmingham City with a fight after the match between the Zulu Warriors and the Green Street Elite (GSE), the name used in the film for the Inter City Firm (ICF).

descriptions: Junior Business Boys is an offshoot gang created from the Zulu Warriors.
edges.evidence_quote: The Zulu Warriors have also seen offshoot gangs created such as the Brew Crew and the Junior Business Boys

descriptions: Aggi Crew and Hype Crew were in conflict over territory, leading to a turf war.
edges.evidence_quote: The power vacuum that this created was quickly filled by Yardie gangs such as the Hype Crew and Mountain View Posse.

descriptions: The Weather Underground (as part of SDS) was in conflict with the PLP during the SDS split.
edges.evidence_quote: the SDS began to fragment and collapse following a split between the group's leadership in the 'National Office', the Progressive Labor Party (PLP) and their respective supporters.

Oda-gumi.description: The Oda-gumi is a yakuza organization founded in 1958 by Hideomi 'Joji' Oda in Osaka, Japan. It became a subsidiary of the Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate in 1964. The group has been involved in internal conflicts and leadership struggles, and has faced legal issues including arrests of its leaders. It remains active as of 2021.

descriptions: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi members attacked Oda-gumi by crashing a car into its offices.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Yamaken faction, Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, deliberately crashed a car into Oda-gumi's offices in March 2016.

descriptions: Oda-gumi is a subsidiary organization of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Oda-gumi would join the powerful Yamaguchi-gumi syndicate as a subsidiary organization under third generation kumicho Kazuo Taoka in 1964

descriptions: Yamaken-gumi members attacked Oda-gumi by crashing a car into its offices.
edges.evidence_quote: The recent split between Yamaguchi-gumi and Watanabe's former Yamaken-gumi faction has again brought the Tsukasa aligned Oda-gumi into the spotlight as members of the Yamaken faction, Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, deliberately crashed a car into Oda-gumi's offices in March 2016.

descriptions: Vigilantes listed as ally in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Main groups within the AFCA hooligan firm are: - Amsterdam Hooligans 5th

edges.evidence_quote: The Kampuchea Revolutionary Army was slowly built up in the forests of eastern Cambodia during the late 1960s, supported by the People's Army of Vietnam

K&A Gang.description: Predominantly Irish-American criminal network based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, founded after World War II. Originally a burglary ring, it later expanded into loansharking, illegal gambling, racketeering, and drug trafficking. The group controlled the city's Irish-American underworld for much of the late 20th century and had ties to various organized crime groups including the Scarfo and Genovese crime families.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The New Jersey faction is allied with the K&A Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Detroit, Gambino, Lucchese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia, K&A Gang

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
descriptions: The Genovese New Jersey faction is allied with the K&A Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno, Colombo, DeCavalcante, Detroit, Gambino, Lucchese (New Jersey faction), Philadelphia, K&A Gang

descriptions: The Green Street Counts connected with the K&A Gang after release from prison.
edges.evidence_quote: After their release the Counts would later connect with the K&A Gang of Philadelphia's Kensington section.

descriptions: K&A Gang members collaborated with the Dixie Mafia in North Carolina.
edges.evidence_quote: would collaborate with the Dixie Mafia

descriptions: K&A Gang had amicable ties with the Genovese crime family's South Jersey faction.
edges.evidence_quote: but also the South Jersey faction of the New York City -based Genovese crime family

descriptions: K&A Gang and Junior Black Mafia were both involved in making Philadelphia the meth capital.
edges.evidence_quote: It is widely held that this was made possible due to the K&A Gang, and the Junior Black Mafia , among others.

descriptions: Members of the K&A Gang and the Scarfo crime family were indicted together for a methamphetamine ring, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, Scarfo crime family soldier Raymond Martorano, Berkery, and dozens of others, were indicted for their involvement in a large methamphetamine ring.

edges.evidence_quote: They were known to have amicable ties to the La Cosa Nostra , namely the Scarfo crime family

descriptions: K&A Gang had amicable ties with the local Greek mob.
edges.evidence_quote: the local Greek mob

descriptions: FIF is the charity organization of JuD, which is linked to LeT.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2019, after the Pulwama attack, the Pakistan government placed the ban once again on Jamat-ud-Dawa and its charity organisation Falah-e-Insaniat Foundation (FIF).

descriptions: The Errol Flynns are mentioned alongside Best Friends as comparable Detroit gangs, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Like other Detroit street gangs, such as their Westside Detroit counterparts in the late 1970s; the Nasty Flynns (later the NF Bangers), and 7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs or the drug consortiums of the 1980s such as Young Boys Inc., Pony Down, Best Friends, Black Mafia Family and the Chambers Brothers, the Errol Flynns grew out of the racial and economic unrest that transformed Detroit in the late 1960s and 1970s.

descriptions: Best Friends rose to prominence after YBI's downfall, indicating a successor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Many gangs, such as "Best Friends", "Pony Down", " Black Mafia Family ", and " The Chambers Brothers ", rose to prominence in the wake of the YBI

descriptions: The Beneduce clan is organically linked to the Casalesi.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Mascolo: assassinato a Baia Domizia il 20 settembre 1988 per non avere ceduto alle richieste estorsive imposte dal clan Beneduce, organico ai casalesi.

edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Mara Barrio 18 , Nayib Bukele , Gobierno salvadoreño , Pandilla Mao Mao , Gobierno federal de los Estados Unidos , Cártel de Juárez , Los Negros , Sombra Negra , Cártel de Tijuana , Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva , Bloods , Norteños , Armenian Power , Latin Kings y Los Del Sur

descriptions: MOD and Asian Crips were in a turf war.
edges.evidence_quote: M.O.D. and other Hmong gangs such as Asian Crips were involved in a turf war with each other, which resulted in more than 120 shootings in the area.

descriptions: al-Jama'a al-Islamiyya is a breakaway group from the Muslim Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: There have been breakaway groups from the movement, including the al-Jama'a al-Islamiyya and Takfir wal-Hijra.

descriptions: MNLA is the armed wing of NMSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mon people have sought self-determination since Myanmar gained independence in 1948, initially under the Mon People's Front and from 1962 through the New Mon State Party (NMSP). The Mon National Liberation Army (MNLA) has been fighting government forces since 1949.

Bergin Hunt and Fish Club.description: The Bergin Hunt and Fish Club was a former Gambino crime family hangout in Queens, New York, used as a front for criminal activities such as hijacking trucks and loansharking. It consisted of two connected brick front stores located at 98-04 101st Avenue in Ozone Park. The club is defunct.

descriptions: The club served as a hangout for the Gambino crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bergin Hunt and Fish Club was a former Gambino crime family hangout in Queens , New York.

descriptions: WolfSSChanze is a splinter faction of Rock Machine.
edges.evidence_quote: a splinter faction of the Rock Machine led by Suat Erköse. The faction changed their emblem in 2022.

Taylor Street Jouster Nation.description: Chicago street gang originally based in Little Italy, founded in the 1950s or 1960s by Pete Bianco. Primarily Italian and Mexican membership, known for medieval knight symbols. Active until 2005, allied with the Chicago Outfit and Gaylords, rivaling several other gangs.

descriptions: North Side Jousters (a branch) became tightly allied with Chicago Gaylords.
edges.evidence_quote: Starting in the early to mid-70s the North Side Jousters became tightly allied with the Chicago Gaylords

descriptions: Jousters were part of UFO alliance with C-Notes.
edges.evidence_quote: In about 1973 an official alliance called UFO (United Five Organisation) was formed among several north side white gangs, including Gaylords, Jousters, Playboys, C-Notes and Ventures.

descriptions: Infobox lists Chicago Outfit as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chicago Outfit , Gaylords and several other white gangs

descriptions: Hanson Park Jousters (a branch) were allied with Cragin Park Playboys through PVJ.
edges.evidence_quote: At this time, the Hanson Park Jousters were allied with a neighboring White gang known as Cragin Park Playboys, through an alliance called PVJs.

descriptions: Jousters had strong unity with Freaks through GFJ alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Jousters also continued their strong unity with the Gaylords, calling this unification GFJ. This stood for Gaylords, Jousters and Freaks.

descriptions: Infobox lists Gangster Disciples as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples , Latin Kings , Simon City Royals

descriptions: Harrison Gents are stated as main rivals of the Taylor Street Jousters.
edges.evidence_quote: The main rivals of the Taylor Street Jousters were Satan Disciples and Harrison Gents.

descriptions: Infobox lists Latin Kings as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples , Latin Kings , Simon City Royals

descriptions: Latin Pachucos took over Hanson Park after Jousters left, implying a conflict or succession.
edges.evidence_quote: The Latin Pachucos now control Hanson Park

descriptions: Jousters were part of UFO alliance with Playboys.
edges.evidence_quote: In about 1973 an official alliance called UFO (United Five Organisation) was formed among several north side white gangs, including Gaylords, Jousters, Playboys, C-Notes and Ventures.

descriptions: Satan Disciples are stated as main rivals of the Taylor Street Jousters.
edges.evidence_quote: The main rivals of the Taylor Street Jousters were Satan Disciples and Harrison Gents.

descriptions: Infobox lists Simon City Royals as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Gangster Disciples , Latin Kings , Simon City Royals

descriptions: Jousters were part of UFO alliance with Ventures.
edges.evidence_quote: In about 1973 an official alliance called UFO (United Five Organisation) was formed among several north side white gangs, including Gaylords, Jousters, Playboys, C-Notes and Ventures.
